2606.16902.title: Binary Tracking for Spatial QA and Navigation with Open Vision-Language Models
2606.16902.paper_summary: This work addresses spatial question answering for service robots traversing long egocentric routes. Given a query such as "where can I find a dry cleaner on the way back home?", the system returns a metric coordinate that downstream navigation components can act on. Prior Spatial Question Answering approaches leverage retrieval-augmented agents built on closed-source models such as GPT-4o for path exploration. However, robots operating in the real world often cannot reliably depend on online closed-source models due to network instability, communication latency, and deployment cost. It creates a need for open-source based Spatial Question Answering approaches that can run onboard the robot, yet prior research in this direction remains limited. This work proposes BinTrack, a simple yet effective, fully open-source spatial-localization agent that leverages the temporal ordering of a robot's trajectory. BinTrack performs a binary search over the trajectory segments between two anchor landmarks identified from a query. It improves overall accuracy by up to 22.8% over other open-source implementations and even matches the reported closed-source model result on the global category of the SpaceLocQA benchmark, the most challenging setting that has so far required strong reasoning agents such as GPT-4o. Furthermore, its optimized inference strategy consistently yields more than a 1.5x inference speedup over previous approaches. Finally, this work releases GangnamLoop, a novel and practical multi-trip outdoor benchmark collected by deploying a real quadruped robot on public streets with the anonymization policy. It revisits the same locations under different outdoor conditions and pairs the robot's low viewpoint with the human owner's. The source codes and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/ndb796/BinaryTracking
2606.16902.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作針對服務機器人在長距離自我中心路徑上進行空間問題回答。<lb/>給定一個查詢，例如「我可以在回家的路上找到乾洗店嗎？」系統返回一個度量坐標，供下游導航組件使用。<lb/>先前的空間問題回答方法利用基於封閉源模型（如GPT-4o）構建的檢索增強代理進行路徑探索。<lb/>然而，實際運行的機器人往往無法可靠地依賴在線封閉源模型，因為網絡不穩定、通信延遲和部署成本。<lb/>這就需要基於開源的空間問題回答方法，能夠在機器人上運行，但在這方面的先前研究仍然有限。<lb/>本工作提出了BinTrack，一個簡單但有效的完全開源空間定位代理，利用機器人軌跡的時間順序。<lb/>BinTrack對查詢中識別的兩個錨地標之間的軌跡段進行二元搜索。<lb/>它在整體準確性上提高了高達22.8%，超過其他開源實現，甚至在SpaceLocQA基準的全球類別中達到了報告的封閉源模型結果，這是迄今為止最具挑戰性的設置，需要強大的推理代理，如GPT-4o。<lb/>此外，其優化的推理策略始終比先前的方法提供超過1.5倍的推理加速。<lb/>最後，這項工作發布了GangnamLoop，一個新穎且實用的多次出行戶外基準，通過在公共街道上部署真實的四足機器人並遵循匿名政策來收集。<lb/>它在不同的戶外條件下重新訪問相同的位置，並將機器人的低視角與人類擁有者的視角配對。<lb/>源代碼和數據集可在 https://github.com/ndb796/BinaryTracking 上公開獲得。
2606.16902.author: Dongbin Na et.al.
2606.16902.authors: Dongbin Na, Chanwoo Kim, Soonbin Rho, Giyun Choi, Gangbok Lee, Dooyoung Hong
